1.title: Understanding Psoriasis Flare-Ups: A Beginner Guide
1.body:
Psoriasis is often misunderstood as "just dry skin." But it is more complex than that.

At its core, psoriasis is linked to immune and inflammatory processes in the body. This does not mean it is something to fear — it means the body is reacting to something, and the skin is one of the places that reaction shows up.

Flare-ups can be influenced by many factors:

• Stress levels
• Sleep quality
• Food patterns
• Digestion and gut comfort
• Skin products and routines
• Weather, sweating, or friction
• Recent illness or infection
• Medication or supplement changes

The goal is not to control every factor perfectly. The goal is to understand which ones matter most for your own body.

A simple first step is to observe, not to fix everything at once. Start with one area — sleep, or skincare, or food tracking — and see what changes when you give that area a little more attention.

This guide helps you do exactly that, one step at a time.

items.a: Psoriasis is a complex condition with no single goly grail. But true, long-term management is absolutely possible. I don't provide medical diagnoses or prescriptions. Instead, this guide shares the exact holistic framework I used to achieve clear skin after years of struggling. Real recovery starts from within. Modern science shows a powerful link between gut health and skin inflammation—when we heal the gut microbiome and eliminate inflammatory triggers, we calm the immune system. This e-book is a lifestyle blueprint designed to help you repair your gut, shift your mindset, and give your body the exact tools it needs to naturally reverse flare-ups from the inside out
No. Always follow your doctor's advice. This guide is meant to support your existing routine, not replace medical care.

items.a: Psoriasis is a complex condition with no single Holy Grail. But true, long-term management is absolutely possible. I don't provide medical diagnoses or prescriptions. Instead, this guide shares the exact holistic framework I used to achieve clear skin after years of struggling. Real recovery starts from within. Modern science shows a powerful link between gut health and skin inflammation—when we heal the gut microbiome and eliminate inflammatory triggers, we calm the immune system. This e-book is a lifestyle blueprint designed to help you repair your gut, shift your mindset, and give your body the exact tools it needs to naturally reverse flare-ups from the inside out
No. Never stop prescribed medication without speaking to your doctor first. This guide is meant to support your existing routine, not replace it.

items.q: What is the difference between the BM and English ebook?
items.a: The BM ebook (Panduan Sokongan Psoriasis) is written in Bahasa Malaysia for the local audience. The English ebook (The Psoriasis Flare-Up Support Guide) is adapted for a global audience. They cover similar topics but are not direct translations — each is tailored to its audience.

Products are recommended based on general wellness categories (skin barrier, immune, gut, stress/sleep) and common nutrient gaps. We do not claim any product treats or cures psoriasis. Always check ingredients and consult a healthcare professional before purchasing.
Yes. Some product links are iHerb affiliate links. We may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. We only recommend products we believe are relevant to the support areas discussed.

items.q: Do I need to buy supplements?
items.a: No. The guide helps you understand what to look for if you choose to try supplements, but many people find benefit from reviewing their routine, sleep, and food patterns alone.

items.q: How do I download my ebook?
items.a: After purchasing on Payhip, you will receive a download link via email. For the free checklist, enter your email on the checklist page and we will send you the PDF link.

items.q: I did not receive the download email.
items.a: Check your spam or promotions folder. If you still cannot find it, contact us with the email address you used.

pages.label: Page 4
pages.title: Daily Trigger Tracker
items: ☐ Stress level today (1-10): ___
☐ Hours of sleep: ___
☐ Water intake: ___ glasses
☐ Any new skincare products? ___
☐ Foods eaten that may be triggers: ___
pages.tip: ✦ Tip: Track for 2 weeks before drawing conclusions

pages.label: Page 12
pages.title: Foods to be aware of:
items: • Dairy — may increase inflammation in sensitive individuals
• Gluten — some people report flare-ups after consumption
• Sugar — can spike insulin and inflammatory markers
• Processed foods — often high in omega-6
pages.tip: ✦ Try eliminating one group at a time for 3 weeks

pages.label: Page 18
pages.title: My Morning Routine:
items: 1. Wake up — drink warm water with lemon
2. 5-min deep breathing (lower cortisol)
3. Gentle skincare — fragrance-free only
4. Breakfast — low sugar, high protein
5. Morning sunlight (vitamin D)

pages.label: Page 24
pages.title: The Shift That Changed Everything:
items: "I stopped fighting my skin and started supporting it. Psoriasis is not my enemy — it is my body's way of telling me something needs attention."
• From shame → curiosity
• From fighting → listening
• From isolation → community
pages.tip: ✦ Your mindset is 50% of the journey
